title: salix × glatfelteri
intro: Salix × glatfelteri, also known as the Glatfelter willow, is a hybrid of the white willow (Salix alba) and the black willow (Salix nigra), and is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to North America. The bark and leaves of the Glatfelter willow contain salicin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, which has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The health benefits of Salix × glatfelteri include reducing inflammation, relieving pain and fever, and providing antioxidant and antimicrobial effects. Herbal preparations of the Glatfelter willow can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can be used to treat a variety of health conditions, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy.
